document.title: Usability Engineering Plan
standards: Common Criteria for Electrical and Mechanical Safety of Medical Devices [Appendix 3]

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability Engineering Plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG, Report No. A-UEP-001 (Rev.3), dated MAR/31/2020.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Plans usability activities for detector HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC, including intended use, user profile, risk permission criteria, validation plan, accompanying documents, and training.
keyPoints: The document is intended to plan the usability of detector HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC.
Normative references include EN ISO 14971:2012 and EN 62366:2008.
HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy and are not to be used for mammography.
Intended users are medical professionals with a professional license who are familiar with the user manual and understand Korean.
Usability validation covers power cord connection, cable connection, main power button action, PC software settings, and x-ray irradiation.

evidence.claim: The document title is Usability Engineering Plan.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Engineering Plan

evidence.claim: The document applies to Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
evidence.evidenceText: For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-UEP-001 (Rev.3).
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-UEP-001 (Rev.3)

evidence.claim: The preparation, review, and approval date shown for the document is MAR/31/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Lee, Dong wook MAR/31/2020 Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin MAR/31/2020 Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu MAR/31/2020

evidence.claim: Revision 3 is dated MAR/31/2020 and its revision content is Correction of audit Points.
evidence.evidenceText: 3 MAR/31/2020 Correction of audit Points

evidence.claim: The document is intended to plan the usability of detector HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC.
evidence.evidenceText: This document is intended to plan the usability of the detector HAD1717MG,HAD1717MC.

evidence.claim: Normative references include EN ISO 14971:2012 and EN 62366:2008.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2012, Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devices; EN 62366:2008 - Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

evidence.claim: Terms and definitions use Common Criteria for Electrical and Mechanical Safety of Medical Devices, EN 62366:2008, and additional definitions.
evidence.evidenceText: For the purposes of this standard, the terms and definitions given in "Common Criteria for Electrical and Mechanical Safety of Medical Devices" [Appendix 3] and EN 62366:2008 and the following definitions apply.

evidence.claim: The detector is intended for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy.

evidence.claim: The detector is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in general purpose diagnostic procedures.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in all general purpose diagnostic procedures.

evidence.claim: The detector is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The patient population includes radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, chest, spinal, abdominal, hand, foot, and other internal organs.
evidence.evidenceText: Radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, Chest, spinal, abdominal, particular, hand, foot and other internal organs.

evidence.claim: The intended user is a medical professional with a professional license who is familiar with the user manual and understands Korean.
evidence.evidenceText: Education: A medical professional with a professional license; Knowledge: Conditions that satisfy both of the following two conditions - A medical professional with a professional license - familiar with user manual; Language Understanding: Korean

evidence.claim: Operating condition limits are ambient temperature +5 to 40℃ and relative humidity 30 to 75 RH % without condensation.
evidence.evidenceText: Operating condition Limit - Ambient temperature range:+5 ~ 40℃ - Relative humidity:30 ~ 75 RH % without condensation

evidence.claim: Storage condition limits are ambient temperature -10 to +50℃ and relative humidity 10 to 80 RH % without condensation.
evidence.evidenceText: Storage condition Limit - Ambient temperature range:-10 ~ + 50℃ - Relative humidity:10 ~ 80 RH % without condensation

evidence.claim: The applied parts classification is Not Applied part.
evidence.evidenceText: 6.1.5 Classification of APPLIED PARTS Not Applied part

evidence.claim: Usability validation is performed to assess and reduce risks from correct use and usability related errors through the usability engineering process.
evidence.evidenceText: Validation is performed to assess and reduce the risk posed by correct use and usability related errors through the usability engineering process of the detector HAD1717MG (HAD17MC).

evidence.claim: Typical intended users for testing are 5 persons.
evidence.evidenceText: Typical Intended Users (Test Staff) : 5 persons

evidence.claim: Risk acceptance zones are Green Zone, Widely Acceptable Zone: Level 1, and Red Zone, Inacceptable Zone: Level 2 through 16.
evidence.evidenceText: Green Zone, Widely Acceptable Zone: [Level 1] Red Zone, Inacceptable Zone: [Level 2~16]

evidence.claim: Risk control alternatives include inherent safety by design, preventive measures for medical devices or manufacturing processes, and information on stability.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Control Alternatives 1 inherent safety by design 2 Preventive measures for medical devices themselves or manufacturing processes 3 information on stability

evidence.claim: Primary motor function validation scenarios include connecting the power cord, cable code connection, main power button action, and PC software settings.
evidence.evidenceText: primary motor function Connecting the power cord; Cable Code Connection; Main Power Button Action; PC Software Settings

evidence.claim: Criteria for successful validation include tests for connecting power cord, checking cable connection, operating the main power button, checking software-detector link, and confirming image acquisition after X-ray irradiation.
evidence.evidenceText: Fit the power cord to the outlet terminal. Check the cable connection. Operate the product by operating the main power button. Check the link between the software and the detector. Check if the images are acquired properly after X-ray investigation.

evidence.claim: Accompanying documents include Risk Management Plan, Risk Management Report, Usability Engineering Report, and TCF.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Plan (A-RMP-5-01); Risk Management Report(A-RMR-5-01); Usability Engineering Report (A-UER-001); TCF (TCF-001)

evidence.claim: If training is required for safe and effective use of a primary operating function, the manufacturer shall provide training materials, ensure materials are available, or provide training.
evidence.evidenceText: If training on the specific MEDICAL DEVICE is required for the safe and effective use of a PRIMARYOPERATING FUNCTION by the intended USER, the MANUFACTURER shall do at least one of the following: provide the materials necessary for training; ensure that the materials necessary for training are available; provide the training.

reviewFlags.reason: No explicit authority or jurisdiction is stated in the source text; source metadata authoritySignal says FDA, while the source path references CE MDD and the text cites EN standards.
Product reference appears inconsistent in one validation sentence: 'HAD1717MG (HAD17MC)' differs from the document header product model 'HAD1717MC'.
Some extracted text appears to contain formatting or OCR artifacts, including duplicated section numbering, 'PRIMARYOPERATING', 'x-ray investigation', and 'particular' in the patient population list.
